verdictReason: 1 critical name registry inconsistency (INV-029 violation) between story-concept.json nameRegistry and world-bible/politics.json faction naming. All other checks passed.

critical.title: Ashborne Legion Name Inconsistency
critical.description: The organization name for Kaelith's former military command differs between the canonical nameRegistry and the world-bible faction definition.

evidence.location: story-concept.json line 83 vs world-bible/politics.json faction FAC-006

critical.impact: This inconsistency creates cascading name conflicts across chapters, dialogue, character histories, and metadata. Any reference to the organization will clash between canonical definition and narrative execution. The chapter-drafter, voice-maintainer, and all downstream agents will encounter conflicting authoritative sources.

remediation.recommended: Update story-concept.json nameRegistry.organizations to: 'The Ashborne Legion (Remnant)'
remediation.rationale: The parenthetical '(Remnant)' reinforces the empire's decay theme and the fact that what was once a great military force is now reduced to garrison posts. This mirrors Kaelith's own obsolescence and adds thematic weight. The faction description in politics.json uses this naming deliberately.
remediation.alternative: Update world-bible/politics.json faction name to: 'The Ashborne Legion' (simpler but narratively weaker)

passes.title: Geography ↔ Culture Consistency
passes.finding: All cultural homes reference verified locations in geography.json. Imperial culture references Aranthem (LOC-008), Border culture references Duskhaven (LOC-001) and Calwrath (LOC-002), Wending culture references forest locations, Rivenmarch culture references freeholder region. Geographic characteristics match cultural descriptions (coastal cultures have maritime elements, etc.).
passes.evidence: Verified 5 cultures × average 4 location references each = 20+ cross-references, all valid

passes.title: Geography ↔ Politics Consistency
passes.finding: All faction operational territories reference verified locations. High Conclave operates from Aranthem (LOC-008), provincial governorships distributed across mapped provinces, Ashborne Legion (Remnant) operates from garrison posts including Duskhaven (LOC-001), fae factions operate across border regions.
passes.evidence: 7 factions with territorial descriptions verified against 13 geographic locations

passes.title: Geography ↔ History Consistency
passes.finding: All timeline events reference verified locations. Siege of Calwrath (YK 736) uses LOC-002, Kaelith's exile to Duskhaven uses LOC-001, god-war climax at Serath Dûn (LOC-013), Quietus location in Unwalked Reach (LOC-012). No orphaned location references in historical timeline.
passes.evidence: 5 major eras + 3 foreshadowing hooks + 2 key figures verified against geography

passes.title: Magic ↔ Politics Power Reflection
passes.finding: Political power structures accurately reflect magic access. Oathbrand is Conclave-controlled disciplinary tool (magic suppression as punishment). Cerith Court enforces binding contracts through magical enforcement. Lirien's kill order is magically binding, not merely legal. Power tiers in magic system correspond to political influence.
passes.evidence: 7 political factions cross-referenced with magic system access methods and power levels

passes.title: Magic ↔ Culture Consistency
passes.finding: Cultures have consistent and differentiated magical attitudes. Imperial: regulated/administrative (magic as specialized skill). Fae: foundational/embodied (magic as core identity). Border: pragmatic/survival-focused. Wending: fluid/non-categorical. Rivenmarch: unstable/fragmentary. These attitudes are woven into cultural norms (marriage customs, social hierarchies, taboos).
passes.evidence: 5 cultures with explicit magical attitudes cross-referenced with magic system practitioner descriptions

passes.title: History ↔ All Modules Coherence
passes.finding: Historical events are internally consistent across all modules. Thalen Reyvas's 40-year poisoning (YK 702-742) is referenced in history timeline, politics faction profile (Sixth Seat, 43 years tenure), and culture (bureaucratic blindness enabling conspiracy). Warden lineage extinction myth is coordinated across history, magic system (kinetic memory), and geography (Quietus location). No contradictions detected.
passes.evidence: Cross-referenced 5 major historical eras against all 5 world-bible files

passes.title: Name Registry 98% Compliance
passes.finding: All canonical names from story-concept.json nameRegistry are used consistently with only one exception (Ashborne Legion - addressed as critical finding). Characters: Kaelith Maren ✓, Lirien Dael ✓. Deity: Ythara, the Unfinished ✓. World Elements: The Pallor ✓, The Quietus ✓, The Oathbrand ✓. Organizations: The Seep ✓, The Cerith Court ✓, The High Conclave ✓, The Ashborne Legion ✗. Locations: Calwrath ✓, Duskhaven ✓. No placeholder names from world-seed detected (no 'The Veil', no 'Loyalty Brand').
passes.evidence: Verified 30+ mentions of canonical names across all world-bible files

passes.title: Romance Obstacles: 7 Structural Layers
passes.finding: The world provides substantive, multi-layered romantic obstacles that serve both romance and theme. (1) Oathbrand erasure makes Kaelith socially invisible, removal requires vulnerability. (2) Kill contract binding prevents Lirien from leaving without executing Kaelith. (3) Fae-human taboo creates social radioactivity. (4) Institutional opposition - High Conclave both branded Kaelith AND issued kill contract. (5) Magical imbalance - Lirien (Tier 4) vs Kaelith (Tier 1) creates asymmetric vulnerability. (6) Duty vs desire paradox embodies TP-2. (7) Necessity vs choice paradox embodies TP-3. These are structural, not incidental.
passes.evidence: 7 distinct obstacles mapped to culture.json social norms, magic-system.json power dynamics, politics.json institutional forces

passes.title: Fantasy Plot Fully Supported
passes.finding: The god-seal quest is comprehensively enabled by world structure. Quietus seal exists with documented constraints (kinetic memory, embodied knowledge). Ythara defined with clear scope (unravels reality into unrealized potential). Stakes explicit (Pallor tearing = god waking = annihilation). Thalen's conspiracy provides urgency. Journey coherent across 8 key locations from Duskhaven to Quietus.
passes.evidence: Verified journey path: Duskhaven → Greymere → Thornway → Veldrane → Wending → Aranthem → Serath Dûn → Unwalked Reach → Quietus. Each location has documented narrative function.

passes.title: INV-009: Deus Ex Machina Prevention (Magic System)
passes.finding: Magic system establishes hard boundaries that prevent narrative escape. 10 comprehensive rules (focused will, exponential costs, Pallor gradient, binding mechanics, feedback loops, resistance, range limits, permanence, compatibility, knowledge transmission). 7 forbidden uses block key escape routes (resurrection, compulsion, transmutation, creation, memory erasure, breaking Quietus/Pallor, non-consensual binding). Zero magical escape hatches available to resolve conflicts without established rules.
passes.evidence: magic-system.json rules array (10 entries) + forbiddenUses array (7 entries) verified

passes.title: INV-018: No Anachronisms
passes.finding: Cultural norms are consistent with world's technology and magic level. No electricity, databases, algorithms, or modern terminology detected. Tech level (medieval military infrastructure, garrison posts, road maintenance) matches magic level (rare, specialized skill). Religion (the Keeping) is pragmatic bureaucracy fitting medieval imperial framework. Social structures (hereditary seats, guild systems, oath-binding) are pre-modern.
passes.evidence: Reviewed all 5 cultures for anachronistic concepts - none found

passes.title: INV-029: Artifact Cross-References Valid
passes.finding: All world-bible files have valid upstreamRef fields. geography.json: 'story-concept.json, world-bible/geography.json'. culture.json: 'story-concept.json'. history.json: 'story-concept.json, world-bible/geography.json, world-bible/culture.json'. magic-system.json: 'story-concept.json, world-bible/geography.json, world-bible/culture.json, world-bible/history.json'. politics.json: multiple valid upstream references including story-concept, geography, culture, history, magic-system.
passes.evidence: Verified upstreamRef field in all 5 world-bible files

passes.title: INV-081: No Darlings Detected
passes.finding: All cultural elaboration serves narrative function. No world elements are elaborate without purpose. Imperial culture (~3,800w) enables Oathbrand taboo + tier system + conspiracy invisibility. Fae culture (~4,700w) enables Lirien's arc + binding paradox + contract framework. Border culture (~3,300w) opens story + garrison protocols + marks Kaelith as border-born. Wending culture (~2,400w) provides space where relationship is unremarkable (Act II internal focus). Rivenmarch (~2,300w) final cultural phase before climax. Every elaboration serves fantasy plot OR romance arc.
passes.evidence: Reviewed all 5 cultures for purposeless elaboration - none found

passes.title: Geography: Sufficient for Journey Narrative
passes.finding: Geography provides 13 locations with documented narrative functions. 8 locations form coherent journey path from northeastern coast (Duskhaven) to far west (Quietus). 5 additional locations provide context (Imperial heartland, river cities, border regions). Travel routes established (The Thornway road). Climate zones differentiated. Each location has enough detail for chapter-drafter to write immersive scenes.
passes.evidence: 13 locations in geography.json, each with 200-400 word descriptions including atmosphere, function, and sensory details

passes.title: Culture: Romantic-Relevant Social Norms
passes.finding: All 5 cultures provide romantic-relevant social norms. Imperial: Oathbrand erasure, bureaucratic invisibility, tier-based hierarchy. Fae: binding mechanics, contract enforcement, relationship as negotiation. Border: pragmatic survival, no-questions culture, garrison isolation. Wending: non-categorical identity, fluidity accepted, no taboos. Rivenmarch: fragment culture, isolation, dissolution of external constraints. Marriage customs, forbidden relationships, and duty vs. desire tensions are embedded in cultural structures.
passes.evidence: Each culture.json entry includes explicit 'romanticObstacles' or 'socialNorms' sections addressing relationships

passes.title: History: Foreshadowing Hooks Provided
passes.finding: History provides 5 foreshadowing hooks with clear plants and payoffs. (1) Warden lineage extinction myth → Thenndal carried knowledge. (2) Pallor natural fluctuations → Poisoning camouflage. (3) Velmere's Renewal Debate → Thalen's methodology. (4) Legion as Watch (originally) → Kaelith's unknowing inheritance. (5) Thenndal's death as 'chronic illness' → Potential Conclave murder. Each hook has explicit plant + payoff structure documented.
passes.evidence: history.json foreshadowingHooks array with 5 entries, each containing plant + payoff + integration strategy

passes.title: Magic System: Rules, Costs, Limitations Clear
passes.finding: Magic system has comprehensive rule structure. 10 fundamental rules establish how magic works. 7 forbidden uses establish what magic cannot do. Power tier system (0-4) establishes magical hierarchy. Costs documented (exhaustion, mental strain, physical pain). Limitations explicit (Pallor gradient, focused will required, exponential costs for divergence, no unconscious magic). Oathbrand mechanics defined. Binding mechanics defined. Kinetic memory mechanics defined.
passes.evidence: magic-system.json contains: 10 rules, 7 forbidden uses, 5 power tiers, 3 major magical elements (Pallor, Oathbrand, Quietus), access methods, hardness rationale

passes.title: Politics: Obstacles and Antagonists Provided
passes.finding: Politics provides 7 factions with competing goals and clear antagonist profile. High Conclave (7 seats detailed, including Thalen Reyvas the poisoner with 40-year conspiracy). Cerith Court (enforces Lirien's kill contract). The Seep (intelligence network). Provincial governorships (institutional inertia). Ashborne Legion Remnant (Kaelith's former command). Rivenmarch Freeholders (fragmentation). Each faction has motivations, operational methods, and potential for conflict.
passes.evidence: 7 factions in politics.json, each with leadership structure, goals, operational methods, and relationship to protagonists

passes.title: Tone Contract Consistency
passes.finding: World-bible is consistent with tone contract from story-concept.json. Primary tone (morally complex, atmospherically taut) reflected in: empire's twilight decay, Conclave's bureaucratic corruption, fae as cold/bureaucratic not whimsical, Thalen's ideological purity not simple villainy. Secondary tone (darkly romantic with dry wit) reflected in: Oathbrand as externalized guilt, kill contract as binding paradox, culture descriptions using restraint not melodrama. No forbidden tones detected (no whimsy, no grimdark nihilism, no sentimentality, no camp).
passes.evidence: Reviewed all 5 world-bible files against 6 forbidden tones from story-concept.json toneContract

passes.title: Thematic Pillar Integration
passes.finding: All 3 thematic pillars are woven into world structure. TP-1 (Trust as structural failure): Kill contract + binding magic creates literal paradox where trust requires vulnerability to someone who can destroy you. TP-2 (Price of right choice): Kaelith's Calwrath decision (tactically correct, morally devastating) + Thalen's 40-year poisoning (ideologically pure, existentially catastrophic). TP-3 (Chosen vs only): Necessity cage (kill contract) must open for choice to become possible - Rivenmarch/Unwalked Reach geography provides space where external constraints dissolve.
passes.evidence: Cross-referenced 3 thematic pillars from story-concept.json against world-bible structure in magic, politics, culture, geography

auditNarrative: The world-bible for story 001 is exceptionally well-constructed with sophisticated thematic integration, comprehensive narrative utility, and rigorous internal consistency. All cross-file references are valid, all name registry entries are compliant (except one), all invariants are honored (except one), and all completeness requirements are met. The magic system provides robust deus ex machina prevention through 10 rules and 7 forbidden uses. Romance obstacles are structural rather than incidental, serving both relationship dynamics and thematic scaffolding. The fantasy plot is fully supported with coherent journey geography, clear stakes, and a compelling conspiracy. Cultural elaboration serves narrative function without excess. Historical foreshadowing hooks are well-planted. Political factions provide obstacles and a nuanced antagonist. The tone is consistent with the story's twilight-empire atmosphere and morally complex framework. The single critical violation is a name registry inconsistency: story-concept.json defines the organization as 'The Ashborne Legion' while world-bible/politics.json names it 'The Ashborne Legion (Remnant)'. This is a technical coordination failure, not a narrative deficiency. Once the name is standardized (recommended: adopt 'Remnant' variant for thematic weight), the world-bible will pass gate without reservations. The worldbuilding is production-ready pending this single fix.

blocking.action: Update story-concept.json nameRegistry.organizations['The Ashborne Legion'] to 'The Ashborne Legion (Remnant)' OR update world-bible/politics.json faction FAC-006 name to 'The Ashborne Legion'

nextSteps: Fix Ashborne Legion name inconsistency (choose recommended option: adopt 'Remnant' variant)
Proceed to character development phase with confidence - world structure is robust

signOff.readinessAssessment: World-bible is production-ready pending single name standardization. Once fixed, gate will pass and character development can begin. The worldbuilding quality is exceptional - thematically coherent, narratively sophisticated, internally consistent, and complete. The critical violation is administrative, not creative.
